zh.Bracewell, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾布拉可維爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard VI Tempest於大約1334年出生在英國越可爾布拉可維爾時，他的父親Sir John Tempest29歲，母親Catherine Sherborn16歲。他於大約1355年與Mary Talbot在英國越可爾布拉可維爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1389年2月16日之前在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Bracewell, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾布拉可維爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard VI Tempest于大约1334年出生在英国越可爾布拉可維爾时，他的父亲Sir John Tempest29岁，母亲Catherine Sherborn16岁。他于大约1355年与Mary Talbot在英国越可爾布拉可維爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1389年2月16日之前在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Tempest
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire): nickname for someone with a blustery temperament, from Middle English, Old French tempest(e) ‘storm’ (from Latin tempestas ‘weather, season’, a derivative of tempus ‘time’).

variants.name: Tempesta
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Raffaele, Agostino, Angelo, Carmelo, Cesare, Cesidio, Dante, Elio, Ezio, Gino.
Italian: nickname for someone with a stormy temperament, from tempesta ‘storm, tempest’ (see Tempest ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. nameMeanings.name: VI
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard VI Tempest was born about 1334, in Bracewell, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir John Tempest, was 29 and his mother, Catherine Sherborn, was 16. He married Mary Talbot about 1355, in Bracewell, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died before 16 February 1389, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard VI Tempest est né environ 1334, à Bracewell, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Tempest, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Catherine Sherborn, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Mary Talbot environ 1355, à Bracewell, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé avant 16 février 1389, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard VI Tempest um 1334 in Bracewell, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Tempest 29 und seine Mutter Catherine Sherborn 16. Er hat um 1355 in Bracewell, Yorkshire, England Mary Talbot geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb vor 16. Februar 1389 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard VI Tempest è nato il circa 1334 nel Bracewell, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John Tempest, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Catherine Sherborn, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Mary Talbot circa 1355, nel Bracewell, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 16 febbraio 1389, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard VI Tempestが約1334年にイギリスヨークシュアイアブレイセウエルで生まれた時，父Sir John Tempestは29歳，母Catherine Sherbornは16歳であった。約1355年にイギリスヨークシュアイアブレイセウエルでMary Talbotと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1389年2月16日以前に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard VI Tempest(이)가 대략 1334년 영국 요르크실르 브라스웰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Tempest(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine Sherborn(은)는 16세였다. 그는 대략 1355년 영국 요르크실르 브라스웰에서 Mary Talbot(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1389년 2월 16일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

mn.about 1334–before 16 February 1389: ойролцоогоор 1334 он–1389 оны Хоёрдугаар сарын 16–наас өмнө

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard VI Tempest nasceu aproximadamente 1334, em Bracewell, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Tempest, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Catherine Sherborn, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Talbot aproximadamente 1355, em Bracewell, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 16 de fevereiro de 1389, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard VI Tempest родился около 1334 г. в местности бреисюелл, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Tempest было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine Sherborn, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Talbot около 1355 г. в местности бреисюелл, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер перед 16 февраль 1389 г. в его родном городе.

sm.about 1334–before 16 February 1389: e tusa o le 1334–a'e le'I oo le 16 Fepuari 1389

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard VI Tempest nació aproximadamente 1334, en Bracewell, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Tempest, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Catherine Sherborn, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Mary Talbot aproximadamente 1355, en Bracewell, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió antes de 16 de febrero de 1389, en su pueblo.
